# PR Review: #33451 - Fix ApplyQueryAttributes called with empty dictionary on back

**Date:** 2026-01-09 | **Issue:** [#33415](https://github.com/dotnet/maui/issues/33415) | **PR:** [#33451](https://github.com/dotnet/maui/pull/33451)

## ⏳ Status: IN PROGRESS

| Phase | Status |
|-------|--------|
| Pre-Flight | ✅ COMPLETE |
| 🧪 Tests | ✅ COMPLETE |
| 🚦 Gate | ✅ PASSED |
| 🔧 Fix | ▶️ IN PROGRESS |
| 📋 Report | ⏳ PENDING |

---

<details>
<summary><strong>📋 Issue Summary</strong></summary>

**Issue:** ApplyQueryAttributes gets called with empty Dictionary on back navigation even after calling `query.Clear()`

**Expected Behavior:** According to [Microsoft's documentation](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/dotnet/maui/fundamentals/shell/navigation?view=net-maui-10.0#pass-single-use-object-based-navigation-data), calling `query.Clear()` should prevent the method from being called again when re-navigating to the existing page.

**Current Behavior:** `ApplyQueryAttributes` IS called with an empty dictionary when navigating back, even after calling `query.Clear()`.

**Steps to Reproduce:**
1. `Shell.Current.GoToAsync(PageA, new Dictionary<string, object> { { "MyKey", "MyValue" } });`
2. On PageA's ViewModel `ApplyQueryAttributes`, do `query.Clear();`
3. From PageA -> `Shell.Current.GoToAsync(PageB);`
4. From PageB -> Either press back button or call `Shell.Current.GoToAsync("..");`
5. You get back on PageA, and its ViewModel's `ApplyQueryAttributes` method gets called with an empty `Dictionary`

**Platforms Affected:**
- [x] iOS (verified in issue)
- [x] Android (likely affected - not mentioned in issue but PR author includes it)
- [ ] Windows (not mentioned)
- [ ] MacCatalyst (not mentioned)

**Version:** 10.0.20, also verified in 9.0.120 and 10.0.10

**Workaround:** Return early from `ApplyQueryParameters` if query is empty, but this violates documented behavior.

</details>

<details>
<summary><strong>🔧 Fix Candidates</strong></summary>

**Status**: ⏳ PENDING

| # | Source | Approach | Test Result | Files Changed | Notes |
|---|--------|----------|-------------|---------------|-------|
| PR | PR #33451 | Add checks to skip ApplyQueryAttributes when `mergedData.Count == 0 && isPopping == true` in both ShellContent path and isLastItem path | ✅ PASS (Gate) | `ShellNavigationManager.cs` (+15 -2) | Original PR - validated by Gate ✅ |

**Root Cause (per PR):**
`ApplyQueryAttributes` was being invoked via **two separate code paths** during back navigation:
1. When `baseShellItem is ShellContent` (lines 313-331)
2. When `baseShellItem` is NOT `ShellContent` but `isLastItem=true` (lines 334-341)

Both paths would call `ApplyQueryAttributes` even when merged query data was empty during back navigation.

**PR's Fix Logic:**
```csharp
// Skip applying/setting query attributes if empty and popping back
if (mergedData.Count > 0 || !isPopping)
{
    // Only call/set if data exists OR not popping
}
```

**Exhausted:** No
**Selected Fix:** [PENDING]

</details>
